Appearances: Lon Lazar appeared by zoom for and with Renard Williams (petitioner) by zoom.
Jonathan Huber appeared by zoom for Bridget Carter (beneficiary).
Trisha Firedeberg appeared by zoom for Leo Bautista (proposed administrator).

Mr. Huber and Ms. Friedeberg stated that they have no objections to the petition for final distribution. The Petition for Removal of Personal Representative and Suspension of Powers, etc., the Petition for Letters of Administration, and the Order to Show Cause are off calendar. The Amended First and Final Account, Petition for Allowances of Expenses and Statutory Fees, and for Final Distribution is under submission.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Estate Petition
Off Calendar Oct-19-2021 Continued To Nov-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-19-2021 Continued To Nov-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Personal Representative And Suspension Of Powers; And To Compel An Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Continued To Oct-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jonathan Huber, via ZOOM, for Bridget Carter, Petitioner; Lon Lazar, via ZOOM, for Renard Williams, via ZOOM, Personal Representative; Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, for Leo Bautista, proposed Successor Executor.

The court begins by confirming with Mr. Lazar that a move-out agreement has been signed by the tenants of the real property. The court instructs Mr. Lazar to file a copy of the agreement. Mr. Huber confirms that he has seen the agreement, but casts doubt that Mr. Williams will be able to close the estate. The court then advises counsel of the roadblocks preventing the resolution of these matters especially regarding Freddie Sr.'s deceased spouse Elizabeth's estate. The court advises the parties that Elizabeth's estate will need to be resolved via its own petition. Mr. Huber reports that he is willing to work with Ms. Navia to file the petition. Mr. Lazar provides oral testimony regarding the history of the relationship between Freddie Sr. and Elizabeth. The court reminds Mr. Lazar that he should have been following the probate code and that these matters should have been resolved by now. The court then confirms with Mr. Lazar that a two-year accounting should be filed in the Freddie Williams Sr. matter to cover the dates of May-15-2018 to May-18-2020.

After additional discussion, the matters in this case are continued to Oct-19-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Status on the Filing of A Petition in Elizabeth's Estate is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Sep-07-2021 Continued To Nov-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-07-2021 Continued To Sep-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Personal Representative And Suspension Of Powers; And To Compel An Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-07-2021 Continued To Sep-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Huber, via ZOOM, for Bridget Carter, Petitioner; Lon Lazar, via ZOOM, for Renard Williams, via ZOOM, Personal Representative; Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, for Leo Bautista, via ZOOM, proposed successor Executor.

The court begins by advising that there may be notice issues. Mr. Huber reports that he does not believe so as Mr. Williams had been personally served with the citation. The court then requests clarification regarding whether the current tenants of the real property have agreed to and signed an agreement to the buyout. Mr. Lazar reports that he does not believe that there is currently a fully signed agreement and that Mr. Williams is meeting with the tenants who have yet to sign the agreement later today. Mr. Williams provides oral testimony with regard to the real property and the history of the matter including the difficulties he has encountered with attempts to having all family in order to move this matter and related matter forward. Mr. Huber then advises that he believes that the cases are ready for distribution and believes that the process would proceed quicker if Mr. Williams is removed and replaced by Mr. Bautista. Mr. Williams then confirms that he consulted with a Landlord/Tenant attorney who drafted the move-out agreement and that listing the property for sale is the best way to move the matters along. Discussions regarding accountings are made with Mr. Huber asking for accountings from 2001. The court advises that his request does not seem practical. Mr. Lazar reports that the accounting should only go back as far as 2018 and that the family was fully aware that Mr. Williams had been collecting rent and depositing the funds into a blocked account. After additional discussion regarding accounting, the court notifies Mr. Lazar that it will be ordering accountings in this matter and related matter. The accountings are to cover two (2) years from Renard Williams' appointment as Administrator. The court also advises Mr. Lazar that if there is no formal move-out agreement from the tenants one week prior to the next calling of this matter, the petitions to remove his client may be granted. Mr. Lazar is also instructed to file a Status Report regarding the preceding by 9/21/2021, one week prior to the next calling of this matter.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Removal, etc., and Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to 9/28/2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Status of two-year accounting is set for 10/19/2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Continued To Sep-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Personal Representative And Suspension Of Powers; And To Compel An Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-20-2021 Continued To Sep-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lon Lazar, via ZOOM, for Renard Williams, Personal Representative; Jonathan Huber, via ZOOM, for Bridget Carter, Petitioner; Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, for Leo Bautista, proposed Successor Administrator.

The court begins by confirming with Mr. Lazar that a Petition for Final Distribution has not yet been filed. Mr. Lazar reports that there has been a lack of communication between the parties and that his client has been working to resolve issues with the real property specifically, negotiating a move-out date with tenants so that the property can be prepared to list and sell. Mr. Lazar hope to have the property listed within thirty (30) days. Mr. Hubert reports that he does not recall an agreement to sell the property. The court advises that John requires a personal representative. The court also advises Mr. Huber regarding the issue with the citation. Mr. Huber reports that he has had issues with court staff regarding the citation and believes that the court can remove the personal representative and appoint a successor. The court reminds Mr. Huber that a citation is jurisdictional.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Removal of Personal Representative, etc., and Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to September 7, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Continued To Oct-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Austin Adams appeared by zoom for Bridget Carter (Beneficiary).
Lon Lazar appeared by zoom for and with Renard Williams (Administrator) by zoom.

The Court noted that the Probate Examiner's notes indicate that the petition must include allegations to support approval of the status report, that it requests a supplement to the petition to address the notice issue under Probate Code 9202, requests for special notice, and allegations regarding notice to creditors and filing of any creditor's claims, and that there is no proof of service of the petition and that no proposed order has not been delivered to the Court. Mr. Lazar stated that an order was provided on Friday, that he was unable to locate the original proof of service, that he contacted Mr. Adams' office, that it is his understanding that Mr. Huber has waived additional notice and that he will file a supplement. Mr. Adams stated that he received a copy of the order, that he will be filing a petition to remove the Administrator and appoint a new Administrator, that the Administrator is failing to account and that the timeline to close the estate is extremely long. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Adminstration is continued to October 14, 2020 at 9;00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-19-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Austin Adams appeared by zoom for Bridget Carter (Beneficiary).

Mr. Adams stated that he has had no contact with any Counsel for Mr. Williams. The Court noted that there are lots of problems with this case and that Mr. Lazar is not present. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Lon Lazar and Administrator Renard Williams to appear on September 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they have failed to respond to the Probate Examiner's notes. The Petition for Approval of Status of Administration is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Authorizing The Personal Representative To Take Possession Of Property
Off Calendar Nov-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lon Lazar for Renard Williams.

Mr. Lazar stated that the there is another related matter, that the Petitioner is trying to get the two estates wrapped up and that the problem is that the Petitioner got an order from the Court in this matter with no authority to take possession of any money or property and an order in the other matter that any liquid assets are to be placed in a blocked account. Mr. Lazar also stated that he is asking for an order allowing the personal representative to transfer money from the blocked account in the other estate to this estate and order that it be placed into a blocked account. The Court informed Counsel that they need to set up a blocked account in this case first and file an 850 petition in Jr.'s estate to transfer assets to this estate and into a blocked account. Mr. Lazar stated that all of the Heirs are the same, that there are no additional new people to notice, that it is going to create additional expenses and delay, that his Client is being punished for things that other people didn't do and questions who this is going to protect. The Court stated that it is going to protect every owner of the property. The Petition for Order Authorizing the Personal Representative to Take Possession of Property is denied without prejudice.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Continued To Dec-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on December 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or, a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
